title: 3-(ρ-Acetophenyl)- 1 -Methyl-triazene-N-Oxide—A New  Reagent for the Gravimetric Determination of Cu(II)
author: N. R. BISWAS
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, Jadavpur University, Calcutta 700 032 

  Manuscript received 4 October 1978, accepted 12 March 1979  

 3-(  Ƥ  -AcetophenyI)-1-Methyl-triazene-N-Oxide as a ligand has been used in the gravimetric determination of 2-30 mg Cu(II). The chocolate brown complex, Cu(C 2 H 10 O 2 N 3 ) 2 , is precipitated in the   Ƥ  H range 5-10, adjusted with aqueous ammonia and is weighed as such after drying at 110-120°. Interferences caused by Fe 3+ , Zn 2+ , Al 2+ , UO 2+  2 , Hg 2+ , Be 2+ , Mg 2+ , Cd 2+ , Bi 3+ , As 3+ , Sb 3+ , Pb 2+ , MoO 2  -  4 , WO 2  -  could be overcome using suitable masking agents. Anions such as F - , PO 3-  4 , As 3-  4 ,BO3- 3, SCN - , tartrate, oxalate and citrate do not interfere. The average relative error is ±0.49% and relative standard deviation is ±0.38%. The reagent has also been used in the determination of copper in alloys.
